_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=58 with 4 verified claims. 'Without published criteria' (MISLEADING — JPN does publish application conditions and checklists) corrected to 'no published substantive success criteria'. 'Foundlings and stateless children' framing softened. Multi-year casework specified to SUHAKAM-verified 11-year case. 'Family Frontiers documented multi-year cases where wait outlasts primary school' replaced with verified SUHAKAM impact framing (no identity documents, school-admission denials, healthcare access).

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'decides each case without published criteria' → 'JPN publishes application conditions but no substantive success criteria' (distinguishes procedural transparency from substantive transparency).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'pathway for foundlings and stateless children' (oversimplified) → 'discretionary route used by stateless children' (foundlings may also use Article 19A or other provisions).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'published outcome statistics are not disaggregated by NRD' dropped from context (Stage 3 couldn't verify comprehensive negative claim).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'multi-year wait times for many applications' specified to 'SUHAKAM has documented multi-year cases, including one 11-year wait'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'discretion was constitutional from the start' simplified to 'the discretion is constitutional'.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Family Frontiers and other advocacy groups have documented multi-year cases where the wait outlasts the child's primary-school years' replaced with verified SUHAKAM-attributed impact framing.
PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): Article 15A discretionary nature; no statutory success checklist; 2024 Constitution Amendment maternal-citizenship; March 2025 Federal Court Suriani Kempe consent order.
